modules: src/environment/round_physics.py (poll_window_s config; _harq_success_at_budget; M_win round-trip budget on both legs)
S6-p0e-poll-window-timeout.design_note: Design judge-panel workflow was attempted but BLOCKED by the monthly spend limit (all 5 subagents failed); implemented solo with the attempt-budget model I had pre-identified as cleanest.
S6-p0e-poll-window-timeout.model: ell_ij(Δ_poll) = succ_req(M_win)·succ_resp(M_win)·(1-p_col_req)(1-p_hd_req)(1-p_queue_drop)·(1-p_col_resp)(1-p_hd_resp), with M_win = clamp((Δ_poll/slot − queue_slots_j)/(req_slots+resp_slots), 0, M) the HARQ round-trip budget fitting in the FIXED window after the M/M/1 wait; succ(·) is the validated FBL/HARQ averaged_link_success, soft-interpolated at the fractional budget (S(0)=0, linear between integer attempt counts).
S6-p0e-poll-window-timeout.exactness_boundary: the linear-from-0 interpolation is the differentiable mean-field SURROGATE for the discrete attempt-fitting (a fractional budget partially helps, consistent with Chase SNR accumulation); the queue delay is the M/M/1 mean. The independent dynamic MC sampling the DISCRETE completion-time process is P0-F (the judge). Known conservative coupling: a heavily overloaded receiver (rho>1) is penalized by BOTH p_queue_drop (overflow) AND the window budget (delay) — distinct physical effects, double-penalized in magnitude; the MC sets absolute values.

adversarial_audit.differentiability: PASS — frac=b-floor(b) carries grad to budget(queue->pi); succ values carry grad from gamma
adversarial_audit.limits: PASS — Δ_poll->inf recovers ell, ->0 gives 0, perfect-link+adequate-window gives high ell
adversarial_audit.source_destination: PASS — budget uses queue_delay_node[dst_c] (the receiver j's wait), correct ownership
adversarial_audit.truth_leak: PASS — M_win depends on load(pi)+config only, no Y*/vote
adversarial_audit.double_count_queue: NOTED — drop(overflow) + window(delay) both bite under rho>1; defensible (distinct effects), MC judges magnitude
adversarial_audit.mc_alignment: DEFERRED to P0-F — under full physics the MC's Bern(ell_poll) already inherits the analytic timeout; P0-F makes the MC sample the discrete completion-time process independently

S7-p0f-window-alignment.honest_finding: the analytic linear-from-0 soft interpolation is OPTIMISTIC below one complete round-trip (gives partial credit where the discrete floor — which needs a full request+response — gives 0). This is inherent to ANY differentiable monotone relaxation of a floor; it is the documented exactness boundary, validated by the independent discrete reference. At the default 20ms window the budget is >= M so the timeout is inactive; the optimistic band only occurs near heavy queue saturation, where the MC (discrete-ish, the judge) sets absolute numbers.
S7-p0f-window-alignment.exactness_boundary: analytic ell(Δ_poll) = differentiable mean-field soft surrogate (mean queue + linear budget interp); harq_success_within_window_discrete = independent reference integrating the floor attempt-fitting over the M/M/1 sojourn distribution; the dynamic MC is the Level-3 judge for the headline.
S7-p0f-window-alignment.adversarial_audit: a 7-lens adversarial verification workflow (collision / source-dest / poll-window / delay / MC-independence / differentiability-complexity / truth-leak), each finding independently refuted, was run over the complete P0-A..F gate — results folded into the gate verdict.

G-P0-PHYSICS-VERDICT.tests: tests/environment/test_p0_physics.py (6) + test_p0e_poll_window.py (6) + test_p0f_window_alignment.py (4) + tests/metrics/test_confirm_time.py (4) = 20 passed; broad physics-dependent regression 94 passed (exit 0)

G-P0-PHYSICS-VERDICT.conclusion: G-P0-PHYSICS is verified clean across 7 independent adversarial lenses + self-audit + 20 unit/integration tests + the 94-test regression. No P0 physics/delay defect remains. The polling-epoch ell(Δ_poll), source/destination accounting, collision self-exclusion, unicast source activity, survival-sum delay, and analytic/MC window alignment all hold with honest, documented exactness boundaries.

Hardened: documented the c+w<=1 precondition + clarified is_feasible (surrogate=training check; MC=headline judge).

modules: src/environment/overlapping_evidence.py (OverlappingEvidenceModel + closed-form correlation + matched-marginal + scenario matrix)
S11-overlapping-correlated-env.model: O_i = Y* XOR B_road(i) XOR B_sensor(i) XOR B_map(i) XOR E_i; q_i=(1+mu_i)/2, mu_i=prod(1-2p); Corr(C_i,C_j)=mu_i mu_j (1/rho_sh^2-1)/sqrt((1-mu_i^2)(1-mu_j^2)), rho_sh=prod_{shared groups}(1-2p). Sensor/map groups are crosscutting spatial bands (overlap road segments) so same-road peers are NOT exchangeable — the D18 fix.

evidence.MATCHED-MARGINAL control: identical q_i,: PASS (1e-12 marginal match; corr lo<1e-9 vs hi>0.05)

S11-overlapping-correlated-env.honest_property: the XOR correlation is NON-monotone in the shared-bit error: it rises then turns over near p=0.5 (a max-entropy shared bit de-correlates). The strength-sweep test is asserted in the rising operating regime; this is a real model property, documented, not a bug.

mechanism_contract.MECHANISM: overlapping common-cause evidence correlation (the structure CDQ 2.0 diversity will exploit)
mechanism_contract.C1_structure_existence: PASS — target = same-road peers correlated via shared road/sensor/map bits; generator = XOR of independent Bernoulli common-cause + node bits; strength knob = corr_strength (strength sweep); absent control = iid / matched_marginal_low (zero correlation); matched variable = the MATCHED-MARGINAL pair (same q_i, only covariance differs).
mechanism_contract.C2_observability: PASS — observable proxies = the exogenous road/sensor/map group LABELS (and spatial bands), never Y*/C_i/the sampled bits; truth/proxy separation tested.
mechanism_contract.C4_causal_controls: the matched-marginal pair + zero-structure control + strength sweep are in place; the mechanism-BENEFIT factorial (CDQ on/off x structure on/off) is Phase 10 / G-CDQ2-EVALUATION (needs CDQ 2.0, Phase 8) — the ENVIRONMENT + controls are ready here.
mechanism_contract.C3_gradient: deferred to G-CDQ2-GRADIENT (the differentiable pairwise-correlation objective uses overlapping_pairwise_correlation_matrix).

adversarial_audit.note: the VERIFY phase hit the monthly spend limit (4 verify agents failed), so the 4 review-phase findings were UNVERIFIED, not refuted. I evaluated each solo; all 4 were legitimate and FIXED + regression-tested:
findings_fixed: [correlation-math, real] the matrix correlation gave NaN at a p=0.5 SHARED bit (÷rho_sh²=0) and the scalar gave 0 — both wrong. Reimplemented BOTH using the direct cross-moment E[σ_iσ_j] (shared groups contribute 1, unshared contribute both signs) — NaN-free and correct everywhere (true corr at shared p=0.5 is 1). Regression: test_correlation_robust_at_half_probability_shared_bit + scalar==matrix generic.

[matched-marginal, doc] the |s_node_new|<=1 guard is actually CORRECT (the true feasibility), more permissive than the docstring's 'p_shared<=p_node_target' (which only holds for p<1/2). Aligned the docstring to the exact |1-2p_target|<=|1-2p_shared| condition.
[matched-marginal, defensive] added upfront [0,1] range validation to matched_marginal_shared (was deferred to a downstream _vp). Regression: test_matched_marginal_validates_inputs.

[truth-observability, real] the correlated_evidence trace sentinel checked only p_road, misreporting False when correlation lives in sensor/map (incl. matched_marginal_high). Added has_correlated_evidence() to both evidence models (any common cause) and used it in the canonical-episode trace (equivalent for the legacy EvidenceModel). Regression: test_correlated_evidence_sentinel_accurate_for_sensor_only_correlation (constraint #13).
adversarial_audit.post_fix_tests: 16 overlapping tests (10 evidence + 6 scenario) pass under -W error::UserWarning; env regression 74 passed.

S12-cdq2-kernel-math.kernel: L = D^{1/2}(I + eta ZZ^T) D^{1/2}, D=diag(a_j=exp(s_j)>0), Z unit-normalised rows [d,r], eta>=0. This is the FULL-RANK diagonal + rank-r correction that fixes the prior round's low-rank L=BB^T CDQ (which recovered ESP only at r=d).
S12-cdq2-kernel-math.algorithm: Normaliser e_k(lambda(L)) via the EIGENVALUES of the per-source SPD kernel: L_sym=D_c^{1/2}(I+eta ZZ^T)D_c^{1/2} (c=a; eig(L_sym)=eig(L)); e_k=elementary-symmetric(eigvalsh(L_sym)) computed by a cancellation-free LINEAR add-one recursion on the (positive) eigenvalues. UNCONDITIONALLY STABLE (e_k is a sum of products of positives — no subtraction) and SMOOTHLY differentiable in a,Z,eta everywhere incl. eta=0 and repeated eigenvalues (e_k is a symmetric function => eigvalsh eigenvalue-backward has no 1/(lambda_i-lambda_j) gap denominators). Mean(c) factored out only to avoid float OVERFLOW (L_sym(c/s)=L_sym(c)/s exactly). [REPLACED the initial matrix-determinant-lemma z-series det=exp(tr log) — mathematically exact & near-linear-in-d but float64 catastrophic-cancellation-prone at wide dynamic range / large eta, see audit below.]
S12-cdq2-kernel-math.exactness_boundary: EXACT k-DPP normaliser/subset/inclusion under the CDQ 2.0 kernel for 0<=k<=d, validated vs brute-force enumeration (<1e-10, incl. wide dynamic range / collinear rows / large eta) and finite-diff gradients (<1e-4). Cost per source O(d_i^3 + d_i^2 r) (eigvalsh of the d_i x d_i SPD kernel — NOT global N x N); for bounded candidate degree d_i<=D_max (comm radius) this is O(D_max^2 . E) = near-linear in candidate edges E. The determinant-lemma z-series (spec §10) would shave d^3->d per source but is numerically fragile (audit-confirmed), so stability was chosen. The determinantal quorum P(m,n) under this kernel (spec §10) and the exact k-DPP sampler are the next sub-slices (S12c/S12d) before the gate closes.

adversarial_audit.note: EXCELLENT catch rate — the verify phase reproduced ALL 5 concretely; complexity-noNxN lens was clean. All 5 FIXED + regression-tested:
confirmed_and_FIXED: [high math-exactness #1] the determinant-lemma z-series assembled e_k = sum_m psi[m] Omega[k-m] in the LINEAR domain; for wide-dynamic-range a (realistic GNN logits [-7,7]) the alternating-sign terms cancelled catastrophically -> negative/NaN e_k -> log(neg)=NaN (97/750 cases bad). ROOT: (I+zD_c)^{-1} geometric series makes Omega coeffs alternate; no scale cures differential cancellation. FIX: replaced the whole z-series core with the eigenvalue route (e_k of eigvalsh(L_sym) via a positive linear recursion — manifestly cancellation-free). Regression: test_normalizer_wide_dynamic_range_no_cancellation.

[high numerics #2] same root: near-collinear rows + large eta -> negative ek_scaled -> NaN (true e_k provably >0, L SPD). FIXED by the eigenvalue route. Regression: test_normalizer_collinear_rows_large_eta_finite_and_exact (eta up to 1e9).

[high numerics #3] same root: a.mean() scaling cannot bound the dynamic range of heterogeneous a -> inclusion marginals went NEGATIVE (and NaN at a~1e12). FIXED by the eigenvalue route. Regression: test_inclusion_heterogeneous_quality_nonnegative.

[medium esp-degeneracy #4] the `if (e==0).all(): return psi[k]` fast path returned a tensor with NO eta dependency -> at exactly eta=0 backward() RAISED (eta sole leaf) / eta.grad was silently None — killing the spec §9 identifiability anchor (init eta~0, LEARN diversity). FIX: the eigenvalue route has no eta fast path; eta enters L_sym linearly so dL/d_eta=D^{1/2}ZZ^TD^{1/2}!=0 and the gradient is live at eta=0. Regression: test_eta_gradient_at_exactly_zero.

[high gradient-trainpath #5] backprop of an inclusion-based loss to logits s was silently wrong (~0.8 rel-err) because src/mainline/symmetric_polynomials.py::_LogAddExp.backward saved CONSTANT softmax weights (no grad_fn), dropping the (analytically-zero-diagonal) e_k Hessian. TWO fixes: (a) the cdq2 eigenvalue route uses a plain linear ESP recursion (_elem_sym_positive, double-differentiable, no _LogAddExp); (b) ALSO made the shared _LogAddExp double-differentiable (save inputs, recompute weights differentiably) with -inf-safe second order (finite far-negative sentinel + both-(-inf)->0 mask, preserving the masked-candidate zero-grad property). Regressions: test_inclusion_loss_backprops_to_logits (cdq2) + test_logaddexp_is_double_differentiable / test_logaddexp_neg_inf_safe_after_double_backward_fix (shared ESP).
adversarial_audit.post_fix_tests: tests/sampling/test_cdq2_kernel.py (20) + tests/test_g2_symmetric_polynomials.py (incl. 2 new double-backward) pass under -W error; targeted symmetric_polynomials-consumer regression 78 passed; FULL-SUITE regression 359 passed (exit 0, 28m) — the shared _LogAddExp double-backward change is non-regressing across the entire codebase (1 pre-existing finite_blocklength warning, unrelated).

covers: heterogeneous determinantal quorum P(m,n) under the CDQ 2.0 kernel (spec §10)
exact vs brute force + principal-minor reference
S12c-cdq2-determinantal-quorum.law: P(m,n) = [z^k x^m y^n] det(I+zLG) / e_k(lambda(L)), g_j(x,y)=p0_j+p+_j x+p-_j y. Evaluator: [z^k]det(I+zLG) at integer grid (x_a,y_b) = e_k(lambda(LG)) = e_k(lambda((I+eta ZZ^T)diag(c))) with c_j=a_j g_j (the STABLE eigenvalue kernel core at deformed quality c); assemble (k+1)x(k+1) logD, remove ONE common offset M=max(logD) (P=N/sumN invariant to a common scale -> overflow-free + exact), exp, 2-D inverse-Vandermonde N=Vinv D Vinv^T, zero m+n>k, P=N/sum(N). sum_{m,n}P=1 by construction (gen func at x=y=1).
S12c-cdq2-determinantal-quorum.free_references: L = B_tilde B_tilde^T with B_tilde=D^{1/2}[I|sqrt(eta)Z], so the EXISTING low-rank bruteforce_determinantal_quorum(B_tilde,..) (subset x 3^k enumeration) and determinantal_quorum_distribution(B_tilde,..) (principal-minor grid) are two INDEPENDENT exact references — both validated <1e-10.
S12c-cdq2-determinantal-quorum.exactness_boundary: exact P(m,n) under the CDQ 2.0 kernel for 1<=k<=d; per source O((k+1)^2 (d^3 + d^2 r)) (eigvalsh of the d x d kernel at each of the (k+1)^2 grid points — never N x N). Inherits the kernel core's unconditional stability (no catastrophic cancellation). Wiring into the canonical episode / dynamic MC (the bucketed cdq2 quorum path, replacing/extending cdq_bucketed_quorum) + the exact k-DPP sampler is the remaining S12d sub-slice before G-CDQ2-MATH closes.

adversarial_audit.per_lens: math-exactness[] (14 tool-calls) · esp-and-numerics[] (18, incl. the all--inf-grid / common-offset concern — clean: the (x,y)=(1,1) grid point always has c=a>0 so M is finite) · differentiability[] (13) · complexity-noNxN[] (10) — ALL CLEAN
adversarial_audit.conclusion: the quorum is a thin, exact layer over the already-audited-and-fixed stable eigenvalue kernel core; reuses the proven Vandermonde interpolation; 0 defects across 4 independent lenses + 8 unit tests (2 independent references <1e-10) + eta=0 ESP collapse.

covers: exact k-DPP sampler for the CDQ 2.0 kernel (dynamic-MC judge)
empirical convergence to the exact normaliser/inclusion
S12d-cdq2-sampler.algorithm: standard Kulesza-Taskar k-DPP eigen-sampler on the full-rank d x d SPD kernel L=D^{1/2}(I+eta ZZ^T)D^{1/2}: eigh(L) -> (1) select k eigenvectors by the elementary-symmetric k-DPP rule (weights=eigenvalues), (2) elementary-DPP sample from the selected eigenvectors. Induced law = P(S)=det(L_S)/e_k(lambda(L)) by construction. No gradient (MC judge). Mean quality factored out before eigh (overflow-safe; the law is scale-invariant — eigenvectors + eigenvalue-ratio marginals are degree-0 in the scale). L is the per-source d x d kernel, never N x N.

evidence.always exactly k distinct candidates in: PASS (50 draws + 4000-config sweep in review: 0 bad-cardinality)

S12d-cdq2-sampler.exactness_boundary: exact k-DPP sampler (induced law = the kernel law by construction); validated by MC convergence to the EXACT (audited) cdq2_enumerate_distribution / cdq2_inclusion (a sampler is stochastic, so convergence is the right judge, not a deterministic <1e-10). Per source O(d^3) eigh on the d x d kernel (bounded degree), no N x N. eta=0 reduces to the ESP elementary-symmetric subset sampler.

adversarial_audit.method: single focused independent review agent (general-purpose, 74 tool-calls, 79k tokens) — line-by-line vs the trusted kdpp_sample + own MC (N=200k, 5 stress cases: generic SPD / eta=0 diagonal / collinear pairs / rank-1 Z with 1e-3..1e3 quality / k==d).
adversarial_audit.verdict: CORRECT — 0 distributional defects. All 6 scrutiny points (eigh(L).V vs U=BVp/sqrt(lam); step-1 DP marginal; step-2 projection+QR; eta=0 sign/order invariance; pos-eigenvalue filter + k>m check; generator determinism / no global RNG) verified correct.
adversarial_audit.low_finding_fixed: [low, non-distributional] the sampler built L from raw a (no mean scale-out like the normaliser) -> eigh overflow margin only at extreme magnitude. FIXED (scale c by mean before eigh; law unchanged — scale-invariant). Regression: test_sampler_huge_magnitude_no_overflow (a~1e200).

G-CDQ2-MATH-VERDICT.adversarial: kernel audit wf_c696e8e5-3d6 (5 lenses, 5/5 real findings FIXED — the det-lemma z-series catastrophic cancellation replaced by the unconditionally-stable eigenvalue route; eta=0 gradient; _LogAddExp double-backward); quorum audit wf_bb1ada4c-747 (4 lenses, 0 findings); sampler review (1 agent, 0 distributional defects, 1 low robustness fix).

G-CDQ2-MATH-VERDICT.deferred_to_phase10: wiring the CDQ 2.0 inclusion/quorum into the BUCKETED canonical path (cdq2 analogues of cdq_edge_inclusion / cdq_bucketed_quorum + a CDQ2 query policy duck-typed into run_consensus_episode / run_dynamic_mc) is G-CDQ2-EVALUATION (Phase 10), where the ESP-vs-old-CDQ-vs-CDQ2 factorial actually exercises it end-to-end. The MATH gate is the exact kernel/quorum/sampler math + gradients, all complete here. (NOTE: the bucketed path will need a>=0 padded-slot handling — the public cdq2_* keep the a>0 contract; padded exclusion is a Phase-10 wiring detail.)

covers: first-order-differentiable pairwise inclusion pi^(2) via Schur complement
no float()/.item()/detached score on the training path
S13-cdq2-correlation-objective.objective: L_corr = sum_i sum_{j<l} pi^(2)_{i,jl} R_{jl} = E_{S~kDPP}[ sum_{j<l in S} R_{jl} ] (expected within-selected-set total correlation; minimising it trains the diversity head Z,eta to avoid co-selecting correlated peers). R from overlapping_pairwise_correlation_matrix (the deployment-observable proxy).
S13-cdq2-correlation-objective.pairwise_inclusion: pi^(2)_{jl} = det(L_{{j,l}})·e_{k-2}(lambda(L/L_{{j,l}}))/e_k via the SCHUR-COMPLEMENT QUOTIENT identity sum_{S superset of T,|S|=k} det(L_S) = det(L_T)·e_{k-|T|}(lambda(L/L_T)) — verified by hand for t=1 (gives sum_{l!=j}det(L_{{j,l}})) and t=2. FIRST-order differentiable (2x2 solve + eigvalsh of the SPD Schur + the positive ESP recursion; e_{k-t} is a symmetric function so no eigen-gap denominators) — deliberately NOT a 2nd-order autograd of e_k (which the training backprop would turn into a fragile 3rd-order op at degenerate eigenvalues).

evidence.C3: matched-marginal pair (same q_i, dif: PASS (|dge|>1e-4, ||dgZ||>1e-4) — correlation gradient reaches + discriminates covariance (marginals identical so the difference is PURELY covariance-driven)

S13-cdq2-correlation-objective.exactness_boundary: exact pi^(2)/L_corr for 2<=k<=d, vs brute force <1e-10 + finite-diff grad <1e-4. Per source O(d^2 (d-2)^3) (a Schur eigvalsh per pair on the d x d kernel — never N x N); polynomial in bounded candidate degree, linear in #sources. The constant-factor O(d^3) all-pairs reduction is a scale-phase (Phase 11) optimisation, not a correctness/differentiability/identifiability concern.
S13-cdq2-correlation-objective.honest_property: the diversity gradient VANISHES against a UNIFORM (exchangeable) correlation: sum_{j<l} pi^(2)_{jl} = C(k,2) is constant, so a constant R gives a policy-independent cost (dL_corr/d(policy)=0). The mechanism only bites when R is HETEROGENEOUS across pairs — exactly the overlapping environment's D18 fix (crosscutting common causes break exchangeability). The C3 test therefore uses a block-heterogeneous matched-marginal pair, not a uniform one (the first audit-flagged test used an identically-zero low arm; fixed).

adversarial_audit.per_lens: schur-exactness[] · gradient-purity[] — both CLEAN (the Schur identity + first-order purity verified)
confirmed_and_FIXED: [medium, tests-only, objective-semantics-C3] the C3 matched-marginal test was VACUOUS: build_overlapping_scenario('matched_marginal_low') returns an identically-ZERO R, and L_corr is linear in R, so the lo arm's gradient was bit-exactly 0 — the 'discrimination' assertions collapsed to a presence-vs-absence check a structure-blind gradient would also pass. FIXED: rebuilt the test with TWO non-zero matched-marginal arms (matched_marginal_shared p_shared=0.10 vs 0.20, identical q_i) AND a heterogeneous (two-sensor-group, block) R + group-aligned Z (a uniform R makes dL_corr/d-policy=0 by the C(k,2) identity — discovered while fixing). Now both arms carry non-zero, genuinely different covariance and the eta/Z gradients differ — a true covariance-discrimination test.
refuted_but_hardened: [low, complexity-noNxN] pi^(2)/L_corr in the un-scaled linear domain could overflow eigh at extreme quality. REFUTED by the verify agent as out-of-regime (no wrong number in the reachable envelope), but HARDENED anyway for consistency with the sampler: cdq2_pairwise_inclusion now factors out mean(a) before building L and computes e_k from the SAME scaled L — every pi^(2)=numerator/e_k ratio is exactly scale-invariant, so this is overflow-safe AND distribution-preserving. Regression: test_correlation_cost_huge_quality_overflow_safe (a~1e200).
adversarial_audit.post_fix_tests: tests/sampling/test_cdq2_correlation.py (8) under -W error; sampling regression (excl. slow MC sampler) 65 passed.

G-CDQ2-GRADIENT-VERDICT.tests: tests/sampling/test_cdq2_correlation.py (8) -W error; sampling regression 65 passed (excl. MC sampler).
G-CDQ2-GRADIENT-VERDICT.adversarial: wf_8f8422d1-5ad (4 lenses, 6 agents): schur-exactness + gradient-purity CLEAN; 1 confirmed test-vacuity (FIXED with a heterogeneous matched-marginal pair) + 1 refuted-but-hardened overflow.
G-CDQ2-GRADIENT-VERDICT.closes: the overlapping environment's Mechanism Contract C3 (the differentiable correlation gradient reaches the CDQ 2.0 diversity params and is identifiable from / discriminates the covariance structure under matched marginals). S15-cdq2-evaluation-factorial.status: GREEN (user-decided Option 1, 2026-06-27): accept the scoped P_correct win per the plan's 'mechanism effective & scoped' rule, with the F_wrong caveat documented (NOT claimed). No F_wrong reduction faked.
S15-cdq2-evaluation-factorial.harness: src/evaluation/cdq2_factorial.py (reusable: observable_group_diversity, run_factorial_cell, esp_vs_cdq2_cell, wilson_ci) + tests/evaluation/test_cdq2_factorial.py (honest invariants) + docs/gate_evidence/macrostate/run_cdq2_factorial.py -> cdq2_factorial_results.json (reproducible evidence).
S15-cdq2-evaluation-factorial.design: FAIR matched-marginal factorial judged by the INDEPENDENT dynamic-MC basin outcomes (headline judge). ESP and CDQ 2.0 share the SAME distance-based quality (ESP == CDQ2 eta=0), so the ONLY difference is the CDQ 2.0 diversity correction (eta=8, OBSERVABLE sensor-group one-hot Z — the actually-correlated group in matched_marginal_high; deployment-observable labels, no truth leakage). N=36, k=3, r_max=14, base_node_err=0.38, corr_strength=0.33. Arms: iid / matched_marginal_low (zero cov) / matched_marginal_high (positive cov) — the mm pair has IDENTICAL marginal q_i. Fixed-link 0.85 (diversity-FAVORABLE ablation, isolates the lever) + full physics. 6 seeds x 3000 trials (fixed) / 3 x 3000 (full), pooled Wilson CIs.

result_headline.scoped_P_correct_benefit: CONFIRMED + CI-SEPARATED + matched-marginal-controlled. matched_marginal_high: CDQ2 P_correct [0.634,0.648] vs ESP [0.618,0.632] (fixed-link, CIs disjoint); full physics CDQ2 [0.638,0.658] vs ESP [0.620,0.640]. iid / matched_marginal_low (no cov): CDQ2 ~ ESP (P_correct CIs OVERLAP, dP=-0.005). overlapping_sensor_source (the plan's primary structure-on, sensor+map cov): dP=+0.0057 fixed / +0.0062 full (positive, scoped; smaller because P_correct~0.37 there, the consensus-failure regime). => the benefit is COVARIANCE-driven and SCOPED to the correlated environment (ESP cannot distinguish the identical-marginal high/low arms; CDQ2 can) — exactly the plan's 'CDQ wins only in overlapping correlation => mechanism effective and scoped' rule + the C4 mechanism-benefit.
result_headline.channel_caveat_HONEST: the P_correct gain comes ENTIRELY from FASTER QUORUM (F_deadline -0.025..-0.028, clearly significant), NOT from the intended correlation-avoidance. F_wrong is NOT reduced — point estimate consistently UP (+0.009 fixed / +0.014 full, borderline CI). So the operative mechanism is 'diversity -> broader spatial reach -> faster quorum', NOT 'diversity -> less redundant evidence -> fewer WRONG decisions'.
result_headline.mechanistic_explanation: fundamental, not a tuning artifact: in a MAJORITY-CORRECT regime, polling diversely INCREASES a node's exposure to the MINORITY correlated-wrong clusters, so it slightly raises F_wrong; it only rescues the minority of nodes already inside wrong clusters. Robust across 3 exploratory regimes (uniform/distance quality, all-group/sensor-only oracle Z, eta sweep up to 10) + the rigorous run; F_wrong increases MONOTONICALLY with eta (eta->0 => CDQ2->ESP => dF_wrong->0), so no eta rescues F_wrong.
result_headline.surrogate_vs_headline_note: the sensor-only oracle Z PERFECTLY minimizes the S13 within-poll correlation cost (upper bound on any trained Z), yet does NOT reduce F_wrong — so a policy TRAINED to minimize the correlation surrogate would not improve (would slightly worsen) the F_wrong basin. The surrogate and the MC AGREE on the SUCCESS metric P_correct (both favor diversity in the correlated env) but DISAGREE on F_wrong specifically. This is the boundary of the correlation-objective's fidelity to the reliability headline (stop-condition #5-adjacent, but NOT a systematic P_correct misranking).

exit_criteria_assessment.CDQ 2.0 LOWERS the wrong/split RELIABILI: NOT MET — F_wrong flat-to-slightly-UP; benefit is via the deadline channel
exit_criteria_assessment.reliability not traded for latency/energ: AMBIGUOUS — CDQ2 trades F_deadline (down) for F_wrong (slightly up); net P_correct up; whether the F_wrong increase is acceptable is an application/research-priority CALL (a wrong consensus is the most safety-critical V2X failure) -> USER DECISION

S15-cdq2-evaluation-factorial.honest_verdict: a GENUINE, CI-separated, matched-marginal-controlled, correlation-SCOPED P_correct benefit (the plan's 'mechanism effective and scoped' rule is satisfied on the success metric), BUT the round's central 'CDQ 2.0 lowers F_wrong/F_split' framing is NOT supported — the benefit is faster-quorum, and F_wrong is not improved. Reported per the plan's 'accept the honest result' rule; the gate verdict (does scoped-P_correct-via-deadline constitute a Phase-10 pass for this round's reliability-first framing?) is surfaced to the user, NOT self-declared.
S15-cdq2-evaluation-factorial.no_shortcut_attestation: no threshold lowered, no scenario dropped, no test changed to pass; matched-marginal control done; old low-rank CDQ NOT used as the ESP comparator; full-physics confirmed the fixed-link direction; the negative F_wrong result is reported, not hidden.
S15-cdq2-evaluation-factorial.harness_tests: tests/evaluation/test_cdq2_factorial.py (6 passed, -W error): observable-Z truth-independence (C2), matched-marginal identical q_i, wilson_ci sanity, basins-sum-to-1, eta=0 MC ~ ESP, harness smoke. NO F_wrong-reduction asserted (honest).

covers: basin headline via MC at scale

scaling.E_per_N: ~9.15 (E grows linearly with N at fixed density)
scaling.maxdeg: 12 CONSTANT across all N (bounded by comm radius => no per-source NxN blowup)
scaling.total_cells_le_2E: TRUE at every N (the bucketed layout)
scaling.runtime_loglog_slope: ESP alpha=0.882, CDQ2 alpha=0.814 (sub-/near-linear; asymptotically ~1.0 — the largest two points (N=5952->9840) give alpha~1.0; small-N fixed-cost overhead makes the overall fit sub-linear)
scaling.CDQ2_vs_ESP: CDQ2 is a ~2x CONSTANT factor (the per-source eigvalsh) with the SAME scaling slope — diversity does not change the asymptotic complexity
scaling.physics_note: timing used link_override=0.9 to isolate the ALGORITHMIC scaling from the FBL constant; the FBL physics is itself per-EDGE (O(E)=O(N) for bounded degree), so full physics is also near-linear with a larger constant.
scaling.no_NxN_structural: grep over src/ for (N,N)/num_nodes x num_nodes/eye(N) allocations => NONE. The canonical path uses only sparse radius graphs + the bucketed [m,w] layout + per-source [d,d] kernels (d=bounded degree).

ood_matrix.fixed_hashes_across_N: protocol_hash / service_profile_hash / physics_hash CONSTANT across all N; scene_distribution_hash VARIES => scaling N is exactly the registered 'node_count' OOD axis.
ood_matrix.protocol_mismatch_blocked: PASS (a non-OOD axis (beta differs) blocks even with node_count registered)

S16-scale-generalization.self_audit: no new math (reuses the audited canonical episode + ExperimentSpec). near-linear honestly framed (sub-linear fit from small-N overhead; ~linear at scale); no-NxN structurally confirmed (grep) not just empirically; OOD matrix complete (registered allow + 3 block cases incl. the unconditional ideal/full guard); fixed protocol/profile/physics across N verified by identical hashes; CDQ2 eta=0==ESP holds at N=300 (not just dev size); basin headline (sum-to-1, P_correct in (0,1)) works at scale via the independent MC (not node-union).
S16-scale-generalization.deferred_note: the per-source O(d^5) pairwise-inclusion (Schur-per-pair) and O((k+1)^2 d^3) quorum are CONSTANT under bounded degree, so scaling in N is near-linear WITHOUT them needing optimisation; an O(d^3) all-pairs reduction is only needed for HIGH-DEGREE dense graphs (not the bounded-radius V2X regime) — left as a documented future optimisation, not a scale blocker here.

covers: memory-driven CDQ 2.0 diversity MC demonstration (honest basin null)
S17-temporal-memory.mechanism: a sequence of T overlapping-evidence epochs with exactly one ACTIVE correlated sensor band; the band persists (persistence=1) or reshuffles (persistence=0, iid-in-time). matched-marginal-in-time: every node has the SAME q_i every epoch (only the covariance / its persistence changes). A CAUSAL EMA m_t=(1-rho)m_{t-1}+rho x_t over the OBSERVABLE per-band proxy x_t estimates the persistent structure and drives the CDQ 2.0 diversity.

contract_C1_C5.C1_structure_existence: PASS — persistence knob + active-band Markov schedule (constant under persistence=1, multi-band under 0); matched-marginal-in-time control isolates covariance from marginal.
contract_C1_C5.C2_observability_no_leak: PASS — observable_proxy is a per-band MEASUREMENT (schedule + noise), independent of any sampled truth (verified: drawing Y* does not change the proxy); the EMA is CAUSAL (m_t has EXACTLY zero gradient w.r.t. x_{>t} — no future leak).
contract_C1_C5.C3_gradient_reachable: PASS — causal_ema is differentiable in x AND rho (finite nonzero grads); the memory-driven diversity reaches the CDQ 2.0 params; no float()/.item()/detach on the path.
contract_C1_C5.C4_causal_control: PASS — matched-marginal-in-time (identical q_i across epochs and across the persistence regimes); a marginal-only policy is blind to the temporal structure.
contract_C1_C5.C5_mainline_consistency: PASS — rho=1 => m_t=x_t (memory OFF) and eta=0 => CDQ 2.0 == static ESP mainline (MC basin outcomes match within noise).

S17-temporal-memory.estimate_level_result: the memory genuinely TRACKS the persistent structure: estimate_quality(EMA(proxy), true_band) is HIGH under persistence (mean >0.3 over 6 seeds) and >0.2 LOWER under iid-in-time — the memory only carries per-epoch information when the structure persists (scoped, matched-marginal-controlled).
S17-temporal-memory.basin_level_result_HONEST: at the macrostate-basin headline (independent MC, T=8 epochs, 3 seeds x 800 trials, fixed link), the memory-driven CDQ 2.0 diversity does NOT beat ESP: mean basin P_correct ESP=0.654 vs memory=0.644 / no_memory=0.641 / oracle=0.634 (persistent) and ESP=0.653 vs memory=0.643 / no_memory=0.641 / oracle=0.644 (iid). memory edges no_memory by ~+0.002 (denoising) but ALL diversity variants are marginally BELOW ESP, and the oracle (concentrating diversity on the one true band) is even slightly worse. => the basin diversity-benefit is marginal/regime-dependent, INHERITING the Phase-10 (S15) caveat; the temporal memory's value is demonstrated at the ESTIMATE/structure-tracking level, NOT as a basin-reliability gain.
S17-temporal-memory.self_audit: causality/no-future-leak, observable/no-truth-leak, differentiability, memory-off==static, matched-marginal-in-time all directly tested (9 tests). The basin null is reported honestly, not hidden; the diversity construction was corrected (the kernel unit-normalises Z, so the estimate must change z's DIRECTION (shared+diverse components), not its magnitude — the first formulation was washed out / degenerate and was fixed before recording).
S17-temporal-memory.scope_note: this slice demonstrates the temporal-memory mechanism on the REGION-CORRELATION persistence structure; the same causal-EMA primitive + matched-marginal-in-time framework extends to effective-sampling drift / load persistence / weak-cut persistence (the other three plan-§13 structures) by swapping the observable proxy x_t — left as documented extensions, the mechanism being identical.

Headline stays the macrostate basin first-hitting via the independent MC; legacy global-risk emission does NOT enter.
G-TEMPORAL-VERDICT.DOCUMENTED_CAVEAT: at the basin level the memory-driven CDQ 2.0 diversity does not beat ESP (marginal/null, even the oracle), inheriting the S15 finding that the diversity's basin payoff is marginal/regime-dependent. The temporal contribution is the structure-TRACKING memory (verified at the estimate level), not a basin-reliability gain.

covers: bucketed CDQ 2.0 inclusion + quorum on the degree-bucketed layout
modules: src/sampling/cdq2_kernel.py + cdq2_quorum.py (optional `mask` threaded for exact padded exclusion)
S14-cdq2-canonical-wiring.padded_exclusion: the bucket slot_mask is threaded as `mask` into the CDQ 2.0 kernel: a padded slot (a=0) gets an EXACTLY-zero kernel row (zero eigenvalue -> 0 contribution to e_k, inclusion 0) — better than the old CDQ's sqrt(clamp_min(eps)) ~eps approximation. NaN-safe in forward AND backward: a zero deformed quality cs feeds an infinite sqrt'(0)=0.5/sqrt(0) backward, so we sqrt a sanitized input (pos=(real & cs>0); cs zeros->1) and re-zero via the boolean factor; and when a WHOLE grid point is all-excluded (e_k=0, e.g. ideal link ell=1 -> p0=0 for all), log(e_k) is clamped (log(0)=-inf x upstream-0 = 0*inf NaN) — both value-exact for any real quorum.
S14-cdq2-canonical-wiring.exactness_boundary: the bucketed wiring is exact (per-source CDQ 2.0 math on the <=2E bucketed layout, no N x N) with exact padded exclusion. The MC sampler enumerates the exact CDQ 2.0 k-DPP law (dev-scale, C(deg,k)<=max_subsets; cdq2_sample covers large degree). This UNBLOCKS Phase 10 (the ESP-vs-old-CDQ-vs-CDQ2 mechanism-benefit factorial) and Phase 7 (the ESP full-physics baseline now shares one pipeline with CDQ2).

adversarial_audit.method: single focused independent review agent (general-purpose, 28 tool-calls, 110k tokens) over 4 concerns (padding/mask exactness, eta=0 ESP equivalence, dispatch/MC, gradient/no-NxN); compared against the trusted cdq_query.py / _CDQSubsetSampler; own numeric reproducers + 200k-draw MC.
adversarial_audit.verdict: 3 of 4 concerns CLEAN (padded exclusion exact + NaN-free for the padded case; eta=0 ESP equivalence to 5.5e-17 with no Z leak; dispatch additive + non-disruptive + MC law correct; no NxN, grads live incl eta=0).
adversarial_audit.confirmed_and_FIXED: [medium] the cs_safe sanitiser covered only PADDED slots; a REAL candidate whose deformed quality c=a*g hits exactly 0 (p0=0 at an ideal link ell=1) still fed sqrt(0) -> NaN backward (reproduced: link_override=1.0 episode -> raw_q.grad/div.grad NaN; standalone quorum at pp+pm=1 -> NaN). The headline path (ell<1) was safe but the ideal-link ablation was not. FIXED at the ROOT: sanitize ANY cs=0 (real or padded) via pos=(mask & cs>0); AND clamp log(e_k) for the all-excluded grid corner. Regressions: test_cdq2_episode_ideal_link_backprop_finite + test_quorum_gradient_finite_when_p0_zero. (Note: the old CDQ's sqrt(clamp_min(eps)) never NaN'd here — CDQ 2.0's exact-no-eps design traded that robustness away; the pos-sanitiser restores it WITHOUT the eps inexactness.)

S14-cdq2-canonical-wiring.deferred: the analytic episode's node-union F_wrong/S_allcorrect headline is the LEGACY metric; Phase 10's factorial uses the MACROSTATE basin outcomes from run_dynamic_mc (the headline judge), which already works for CDQ2Policy (basin sum-to-1 tested). The bucketed quorum builds [m,Q,w,w] eigvalsh x (k+1)^2 grid — fine at dev scale; the all-pairs/scale optimisation is Phase 11.
